title: Go SQLite connection owner
scope: Go runtime now opens copied/local SQLite databases through a connection owner that configures WAL, busy timeout, read-only query_only mode, explicit write mode, and a transaction helper. This is local/copied-DB runtime plumbing only; it does not initialize a fresh database, run migrations, promote live Go route ownership, or change Pi/Caddy/systemd/frontend defaults.

sqlite_owner.connection_pool_policy: database/sql default pool; connection-level PRAGMAs are supplied through modernc.org/sqlite _pragma DSN values
sqlite_owner.write_mode: PRAGMA query_only = OFF only when DB-write candidate flags are enabled

transaction_helper.read_only_rejection: SQLite write transaction requires write mode

runtime_evidence: Go unit tests verify WAL and busy_timeout are configured on the owner connection and separate sql.Conn handles.
Go unit tests verify read-only owner rejects transactions and direct DB writes through query_only.
Go unit tests verify write-mode transaction commit persists one tag and rollback leaves no partial tag.
boundaries: Does not implement Go fresh DB init.
Does not implement Go migration runner or Schema_Meta writes.
Does not promote Go writes/files to live/default ownership.
Does not deploy to Pi, edit Caddy, change systemd, change frontend default routing, or remove Python.
